rpcs.name: MAGTP USER
rpcs.description: Initialize session (Version Check, Display Check, collect User Info).

rpcs.name: DSIF ICD IMP DATE
rpcs.description: This RPC will retrieve the ICD code system implementation date.

rpcs.name: DSIF ICD GET LONG DESC
rpcs.description: This RPC will return the long description (max 245 characters) for the  specified code.

rpcs.name: DSIF ICD LEX SEARCH
rpcs.description: This RPC returns the ICD-10-CM category and code results of a search based upon a description or code value. The return is designed for display in a tree view control.

rpcs.name: DSIF INP VERIFY PAYMENT CODES
rpcs.description: This RPC will take in a 7078/583 pointer for an inpatient claim to  determine whether the inputted ICD-9 or ICD-10 Diagnosis and Procedure  codes are valid. It will return the inputted codes in as an array with  either the internal number (for valid) or -1 (for invalid) or -2 (for  inactive) for the

rpcs.name: DVBA CAPRI GETSPCLCONSID
rpcs.description: This RPC returns an array of special considerations linked to a 2507  request.

rpcs.name: DVBA CAPRI LISTSPCLCONSID
rpcs.description: This RPC returns an array (listing) of valid special considerations that  can be linked to a 2507 request.

rpcs.name: DVBA CAPRI SETSPCLCONSID
rpcs.description: This RPC sets the passed-in special considerations and links them to the  passed-in 2507 request.

rpcs.name: DVBA CAPRI LISTINSUFRSN
rpcs.description: This RPC returns a list (array) of valid Insufficient Reasons that can be  linked to a 2507 exam.

rpcs.name: DVBA CAPRI LISTCLAIMTYPE
rpcs.description: This RPC returns a list (array) of valid Claim Types

rpcs.name: DVBA CAPRI GETCLAIMTYPE
rpcs.description: This RPC returns a list (array) of Claim Types associated with a valid  2507 Request

rpcs.name: DVBA CAPRI SETCLAIMTYPE
rpcs.description: This RPC returns the status of Claim Types passed to be set to a valid  2507 Request

rpcs.name: DVBA CAPRI GETCANCELREASON
rpcs.description: This returns an array of active 2507 EXAM Cancellation Reasons. New  active reasons added with Patch DVB*2.7*189. Old list of cancellation  reasons set to inactive.

rpcs.name: DSIV ACTIVE USER
rpcs.description: This will determine if a user (DUZ value) is valid and is active.  Also,  additionally screening logic can be passed in to validate a user.

rpcs.name: DSIV XPAR GET VALUE
rpcs.description: This will return the value of a single entity/param/instance.

rpcs.name: DSIV LOCK
rpcs.description: Lock or unlock a global reference from a GUI client

rpcs.name: DSIV SEND ALERT
rpcs.description: RPC to send a user a kernal alert

rpcs.name: DSIV VA DEFAULT SITE
rpcs.description: This will return the name and station number for a medical center division.  Optional input parameters are allowed.  However, if not passed then return the data for the primary division (or institution) as of TODAY.

rpcs.name: DSIV USER DEF DIV
rpcs.description: This will return the default division for a user.  If no default is indicated in the DIVISION multiple, then the return value will be:     1. if the user has only one division, and that division is not      explicitly marked as NO for default division, then return that      division   2. else, if th

rpcs.name: DSIV SEND MAIL MSG
rpcs.description: This will send a Mailman message

rpcs.name: DSIV XPAR ADD
rpcs.description: This RPC will add a new parameter value for an entity.

rpcs.name: DSIV XPAR ADD WP
rpcs.description: This RPC will allow the addition of a new Word-processing type system parameter.

rpcs.name: DSIV XPAR DEL ALL
rpcs.description: This is used to delete the value for all instances of a parameter for a given entity

rpcs.name: DSIV XPAR DEL
rpcs.description: Delete an existing parameter for an entity for a specific instance

rpcs.name: DSIV XPAR REPLACE INST
rpcs.description: For an existing entity/parameter/instance, this will change the value of that instance

rpcs.name: DSIV XPAR EDIT
rpcs.description: This is used to change the value or delete an existing parameter value.

rpcs.name: DSIV XPAR GET ALL
rpcs.description: This will return all entities for a given parameter and instance

rpcs.name: DSIV XPAR GET WP
rpcs.description: This will return text for a word processing type parameter for an entity and instance.

rpcs.name: DSIV XPD VERSION
rpcs.description: This will use the Kernel API to determine the current version of an application.

rpcs.name: DSIV XPD LAST INSTALL
rpcs.description: This returns the latest installed version of a KIDS build.  It gets the data from the INSTALL file.

rpcs.name: DSIV XPAR MULT ACTION
rpcs.description: This is a multi-function front end to the Parameter APIs.  It allows you with a single RPC call to take edit type actions on several parameter/entity/instance combinations.   You can only do these actions:  add, change, or delete   Each entry in the passed list is treated as if it was a separate RPC

rpcs.name: DSIV XPD INSTALL LIST
rpcs.description: This will return a list of all the entries in the INSTALL file.  You pass in either a KIDS Build name or the name portion of the Build name (a Build name without the version number or patch number).  All Installs matching that name will be returned in reverse chronological order (the newest first).

rpcs.name: DSIV ACTIVE USER LIST
rpcs.description: This will return a list of active users matching the lookup value. You  can pass in additional screening logic if so desired, such as security  keys.

rpcs.name: DSIV DATE CONVERT
rpcs.description: This will take a date in any format and convert it to any other format.

rpcs.name: DSIV DPT GET DEMO
rpcs.description: This will return specific patient demographic elements for a patient.

rpcs.name: DSIV FM LIST
rpcs.description: The RPC provides a wrapper around the LIST^DIC API. It exposes more of  the functionality of the API than the DSIV DDR LISTER RPC.   For a lookup value, return all entries starting from that lookup value  and which collates after that lookup value.

rpcs.name: DSIV FM GET FIELD ATTRIB
rpcs.description: This will return the inputted field attributes for a file.    As of 7/29/2003, these attributes are available:  AUDIT  AUDIT CONDITION  COMPUTE ALGORITHM  COMPUTED FIELDS USED  DATE FIELD LAST EDITED  DECIMAL DEFAULT  DELETE ACCESS  DESCRIPTION  FIELD LENGTH  GLOBAL SUBSCRIPT LOCATION  HELP-PROMPT

rpcs.name: DSIV FM FIND
rpcs.description: This provides a wrapper around the FIND^DIC API.  It exposes more of the  functionality of the API to the RPC than the old DSIC DDR FINDER RPC.    For a lookup value, this RPC will return all matches.  It allows for  input a multiple screening logic which would be ANDed together.

rpcs.name: DSIV DDR WP FILER
rpcs.description: This will file the text into the specified word processing field.

rpcs.name: DSIV DDR UPDATE SUBFILE
rpcs.description: This will add a new record to a multiple in a file.   The multiple can be  at any level as long as the IENS is properly defined.  Additional fields  can filed with the record.

rpcs.name: DSIV DDR LISTER
rpcs.description: This calls the Fileman lister, LIST^DIC to get a list of records matching  the input value

rpcs.name: DSIV DDR GETS ENTRY DATA
rpcs.description: This calls GETS^DIQ to get a list of field values for a record

rpcs.name: DSIV DDR FINDER
rpcs.description: Call FIND^DIC to get a list of matches on the file for the input value

rpcs.name: DSIV DDR FILER
rpcs.description: This calls FILE^DIE to file data.  It can only file data at a single  level (top, multiple, a multiple within a multiple, etc.).  It presumes  you know how to set Param[x] properly in Fileman syntax.

rpcs.name: DSIV DDR DELETE ENTRY
rpcs.description: This will delete a record from a file or subfile.

rpcs.name: DSIV DDR UPDATE FILE
rpcs.description: This will add a new record to a file, but only at the top level of the  file.   It does not allow for adding a new record to a multiple.  Additional fields to be filed at the same time can be passed.

rpcs.name: DSIV FM GET FIELD ATTRIB MULT
rpcs.description: This will return field attributes from a selected file for one or more  fields.

rpcs.name: DSIV FM FILER
rpcs.description: This invokes the Fileman filer to update records for an existing entry. This will allow you to update any field at the level of the FILE including word processing fields.  It does not allow for updating different levels of the file.  If you wish to update a subfile, then you will have to make multip

rpcs.name: DSIV LIMIT EIV
rpcs.description: This RPC returns the value of field #62.01 (LIMIT OF EIV FIELDS?) from  file #350.9 (IB SITE PARAMETERS).  This is to allow the GUI to know how  many characters are allowed to be entered for SUBSCRIBER ADDRESS LINE 1  and SUBSCRIBER ADDRESS LINE 2.

rpcs.name: VPS GET CLINICAL REMINDERS
rpcs.description: Called by the Vetlink Kiosk system. The RPC returns the DUE NOW Clinical  Reminders for the patient.

rpcs.name: VPS PATIENT WRISTBAND PRINT
rpcs.description: This RPC prints patient wristband to VistA printer using VistA standard pat Patient Wristband Print routine (DGPWB).

rpcs.name: VPS PRINT PATIENT LABEL
rpcs.description: This RPC prints patient label using the standard VistA Print Patient Label routine (DGPLBL).

rpcs.name: VPS ENHANCED GET PATIENT DEMO
rpcs.description: This RPC is called  by the Vetlink Kiosk (point of service) system.   Given Patient SSN or DFN or ICN or VIC/CAC, this RPC returns the patient demographics,insurance,and up-coming appointments, etc for selected categories

rpcs.name: VPS GET PRINTERS
rpcs.description: This RPC returns up to 20 entries from the device file based on Input Criteria.

rpcs.name: VPS GET ALL CLINICS
rpcs.description: This RPC returns a list of hospital locations (defined in the HOSPITAL LOCATION file), which conform the criteria.

rpcs.name: VPS GET APPOINTMENTS
rpcs.description: This RPC will return appointments for a given data range for a specific Appointment Queue. This RPC will refresh an existing queue with the current appointments and typically need to be called only once a day.

rpcs.name: VPS GET CHANGED APPOINTMENTS
rpcs.description: This RPC will only return new, modified, or deleted appointments and should be called periodically by VetLink to update the appointment queue.

rpcs.name: VPS SAVE CLINICAL SURVEY
rpcs.description: This RPC will return success flag (1) or error flag (-1^error message) VPSDFN  : Patient IEN VPSDATA : Array of field-value pair to store      Format : VPSDATA(1..n)=FIELD-NAME^SEQ#^FIELD-VALUE      Example:                         Survey Name             VPSDATA(1) = NAME^^Survey #1

rpcs.name: VPS GET SURVEY DATA
rpcs.description: Retrieves an array containing the patient's Clinical Survey information.

rpcs.name: VPS GET ALLERGIES
rpcs.description: Called by the Vetlink Kiosk system.   VPS GET ALLEGIES retrieves patient  allergy data stored in an entry or entries in the PATIENT ALLERGIES file  (120.8).  The retrieved data is made available to the Vetlink system.

rpcs.name: VPS WRITE MRAR PDO
rpcs.description: Called by the Vetlink Kiosk system.  The RPC will accept 4 input  parameters; and will return output when processing completes or ends with  an exception.  Vetlink Kiosk has the Medication Review Allergy Review  modules where a  veteran can review and make changes to his medications and any allergie

rpcs.name: VPS WRITE KIOSK PARAMETERS
rpcs.description: Called by the VetLink Kiosk system. This RPC will accept three input parameters and will return result output when processing complete. The input parameters are Kiosk Group (literal value) and the changed  configuration values (array)   Kiosk Group: when an administrator of VetLink Kiosk is making a

rpcs.name: VPS GET MRAR PDO
rpcs.description: Software that supports this procedure will build a global array with each  subscript in the array assigned a line of text representing clinical data  (allergy and medications) associated with the results of a Medication  Review Allergy Review with the veteran.  This is referred to as PDO output and

rpcs.name: VPS UPDATE LAST MRAR TIU IEN
rpcs.description: This RPC will update the last Allergy and Modication review (MRAR) for a patient with TIU Document IEN. The last MRAR is defined as the last transaction date for a patient in file 853.5 (VPS MRAR PDO)

rpcs.name: VPS GET LAST MRAR
rpcs.description: Given Patient ID (in ID/Type pair), this RPC return the last Medication  and Allergy Review data for the patient.

rpcs.name: DENTVX2 GETWP
rpcs.description: This RPC takes in an entity, parameter name, and instance number and  returns the word processing information for the given instance of the  parameter.

rpcs.name: DENTVX2 FILEWP
rpcs.description: This RPC takes in an entity, parameter name, instance number, and the  data to be filed.

rpcs.name: DENTVGUI VERSION GET
rpcs.description: This is an RPC which returns the current valid DRM Plus GUI version and  the current valid KID patch number.

rpcs.name: DENTVICD ACTICD
rpcs.description: Verify that an ICD code is active as of a certain date

rpcs.name: DENTVICD ICD GET DIAG
rpcs.description: This will return specific information for a diagnosis code

rpcs.name: DENTVICD LIST
rpcs.description: This will return a list of active ICD codes for a lookup value.

rpcs.name: DENTVICD ICD10 ACTIVATION DATE
rpcs.description: This RPC will return the activation date of ICD-10.

rpcs.name: XUS IAM ADD USER
rpcs.description: RPC ICR #6290 - API ICR #none This restricted RPC is used exclusively by the Identity and Access  Management (IAM) Provisioning application to add a user to the VistA NEW PERSON file (#200).   The XUSPF200 Security Key is required to add a user without an SSN (file #200 special privileges).

rpcs.name: XUS IAM EDIT USER
rpcs.description: RPC ICR #6291 - API ICR #none This restricted RPC is used exclusively by the Identity and Access  Management (IAM) Provisioning application to edit an existing user in the VistA NEW PERSON file (#200).   The XUSHOWSSN Security Key is required to edit Personally Identifiable Information (PII) such as

rpcs.name: XUS IAM FIND USER
rpcs.description: RPC ICR #6288 - API ICR #none This restricted RPC is used exclusively by the Identity and Access  Management (IAM) Provisioning application to find a list of users that  satisfy a collection of input criteria.   One or more of the input array values must be set by the calling  application. The XUSHO

rpcs.name: XUS IAM DISPLAY USER
rpcs.description: RPC ICE #6289 - API ICR #none This restricted RPC is used exclusively by the Identity and Access  Management (IAM) Provisioning application to display a VistA user.   The XUSHOWSSN Security Key is required to display Personally Identifiable Information (PII) such as Social Security Number (SSN) or D

rpcs.name: XUS ESSO VALIDATE
rpcs.description: RPC ICR #6295 - API ICR #none This API/RPC uses the VA Identity and Access Management (IAM) SAML token definition version 1.2 attributes from a SAML token for user sign-on.

rpcs.name: XUS IAM BIND USER
rpcs.description: RPC ICR #6294 - API ICR #none This restricted RPC is used exclusively by the Identity and Access  Management (IAM) Binding application to set the Security ID (SecID) and  Active Directory UPN (ADUPN) in the VistA NEW PERSON file (#200) for Single Sign-On Internal (SSOi).

rpcs.name: MPI TK POC USER SETUP
rpcs.description: This remote procedure call (RPC) is invoked when a VistA user uses the  option MPIF TK POC USER SETUP to confirm their traits and establish a TK  POC record on the MPI in the New Person file (#200).

rpcs.name: DSIRRPT6 RQSTRS REPORT
rpcs.description: This RPC will return entries from the DSIR NEW REQUESTORS file  (#19620.12) in alphabetical order.  The report can be pulled for all  requestors or in an alphabetic range, and further sorted by those flagged  as Active, Inactive or Both.

rpcs.name: DSIROI9 INACTRQR INACTIVATE
rpcs.description: This RPC should be used to toggle the Inactive field of the DSIR NEW  REQUESTOR file (#19620.12).

rpcs.name: DVBA CAPRI GETCONTREMARKS
rpcs.description: This gets the remarks sent to the contractor concerning the 2507 REQUEST  in file 396.3. The remarks are stored in the word processing field #103 of the 2507 EXAM file 396.4.

rpcs.name: DVBA CAPRI SETCONTREMARKS
rpcs.description: The RPC sets remarks sent to the contractor into the new word processing field #103 of the 2507 EXAM file 396.4. The 2507 EXAMs are connected to the 2507 REQUEST file via a pointer.

rpcs.name: SCMC FILER
rpcs.description: Generic call to FILE^DIE and UPDATE^DIE Fileman DBS APIs.

rpcs.name: SCMC GETS ENTRY DATA
rpcs.description: Calls Fileman DBS API - GETS^DIQ

rpcs.name: SCMC LISTER
rpcs.description: Calls Fileman DBS API - LIST^DIC

rpcs.name: SCMC FINDER
rpcs.description: Calls Fileman DBS API - FIND^DIC

rpcs.name: SCMC GET PATIENT ENCOUNTERS
rpcs.description: This RPC will return an array of patient encounter data. See return  parameter description below.

rpcs.name: SCMC GET NEW PERSON
rpcs.description: This RPC provides the functionality to query the New Person (#200) file. The calling application may query the New Person (#200) file by using  either the VPID or IEN of the requested entry or part or all of a last name. Other optional parameters may be passed to the call as additional filters.

rpcs.name: SCMC PATIENT DIEDON
rpcs.description: Get patient date of death.

rpcs.name: SCMC PATIENT INFO
rpcs.description: This RPC returns patient demographic data.

rpcs.name: SCMC PCDETAIL
rpcs.description: Returns detailed PACT and other team assignment information for a patient. Used to batch load CPRS PC Window Cache during PCMMR deployment.

rpcs.name: SCMC FTEE CREATE
rpcs.description: This RPC will create an FTEE history record in the Position Assignment  History File (404.52).

rpcs.name: SCMC FTEE READ
rpcs.description: This RPC will read an FTEE history record from the Position Assignment History File (404.52).

rpcs.name: SCMC FTEE UPDATE
rpcs.description: This RPC will update an FTEE history record in the Position Assignment History File (404.52).

rpcs.name: SCMC FTEE DELETE
rpcs.description: This RPC will delete an FTEE history record from the Position Assignment History File (404.52).

rpcs.name: SCMC FTEE READALL
rpcs.description: This RPC will read all FTEE history records from the Position Assignment History File (404.52).

rpcs.name: DSIF GET ADJUSTMENT REASONS
rpcs.description: This will return all of the values found within the ADJUSTMENT REASONS  file (#161.91).

rpcs.name: DSIF GET REMITTANCE REMARKS
rpcs.description: This RPC returns all of the Remittance Remarks (file #161.93).

rpcs.name: DSIF GET ADJUSTMENT GROUP
rpcs.description: This RPC will return the Adjustment Group (file #161.92) values.

rpcs.name: DSIF WIN PRINT 7079
rpcs.description: This RPC will create a 120 character wide array of text to duplicate the  printing of a 7079 through VistA so that it may be printed via the  Windows client.

rpcs.name: DSIF INP 7078 WIN PRT
rpcs.description: This RPC will format the 7078 form in a printable fashion to be used  through a Windows (client) printer, instead of through VistA.

rpcs.name: DSIF BATCH LIST AGED REJECTS
rpcs.description: This RPC returns batch IENs (file #161.7) that were transmitted more than  10 days ago and have pending rejects.

rpcs.name: DSIF VEN GET ACTIVE IPAC
rpcs.description: This RPC will return all active IPAC agreements for the specified vendor.

rpcs.name: DSIF BATCH INQUIRY
rpcs.description: Returns the batch status for the selected batch. This RPC is different  than DSIF BATCH STATUS, as it will return the batch status for any  selected batch and not require the batch be opened by the user or the  user needing to be a supervisor to see.

rpcs.name: IBCN SSVI ENABLED
rpcs.description: This RPC checks to see if the System Sharing Verified Insurance (SSVI) is  enabled at a patients treating facility.

rpcs.name: IBCN SSVI SEND
rpcs.description: This RPC sends a patient's active insurace once verified to the patient's  other treating facilities.

rpcs.name: VPS RETRIEVE PATIENT VITALS
rpcs.description: This RPC is called  by the Vetlink Kiosk (point of service) system. This RPC returns the patient vitals information for as given patient.

rpcs.name: VPS STORE VITALS
rpcs.description: This RPC is called  by the Vetlink Kiosk (point of service) system. This RPC stores the patient vitals sent from the Kiosk.

rpcs.name: HMP PATIENT ACTIVITY
rpcs.description: This remote procedure returns all JSON messages for entries that exist in  the cross reference ^HMP(800001.5,"PTAPPT,"HMP"

rpcs.name: HMP APPOINTMENTS
rpcs.description: This RPC finds a list of patients that have scheduled appointments during the requested timeframe, as XML in ^TMP($J,"HMP",n).

rpcs.name: HMP DATA VERSION
rpcs.description: This RPC returns the current version of the XML returned by the RPC 'HMP GET PATIENT DATA.'

rpcs.name: HMP DELETE OBJECT
rpcs.description: This RPC receives a Uid from the client and deletes the object from the HMP Object file #800000.11.

rpcs.name: HMP GET CHECKSUM
rpcs.description: This RPC retrieves the requested data from VistA and returns its checksum.

rpcs.name: HMP GET OBJECT
rpcs.description: This RPC retrieves the requested data from VistA, and returns it in ^TMP("HMP",$J,n) as JSON.

rpcs.name: HMP GET OPERATIONAL DATA
rpcs.description: This RPC retrieves the requested data from VistA, and returns it in ^TMP("HMP",$J,n) as JSON.

rpcs.name: HMP GET PATIENT DATA
rpcs.description: This RPC retrieves the requested data from VistA, and returns it in ^TMP("HMP",$J,n) as XML.

rpcs.name: HMP GET PATIENT DATA JSON
rpcs.description: This RPC retrieves the requested data from VistA, and returns it in ^TMP("HMP",$J,n) as JSON.

rpcs.name: HMP GET REFERENCE DATA
rpcs.description: This RPC retrieves the requested data from VistA, and returns it in ^TMP("HMP",$J,n) as JSON.

rpcs.name: HMP INPATIENTS
rpcs.description: This RPC finds a list of patients that are currently admitted, as XML in ^TMP($J,"HMP",n).

rpcs.name: HMP PUT DEMOGRAPHICS
rpcs.description: This RPC receives updated phone numbers from the client and calls VAFCPTED to save them in the Patient file #2.

rpcs.name: HMP PUT OBJECT
rpcs.description: This RPC receives data from the client and saves it in the HMP Object file #800000.11 as JSON.

rpcs.name: HMP PUT PATIENT DATA
rpcs.description: This RPC receives data from the client and saves it in the HMP Patient Object file #800000.1 as JSON.

rpcs.name: HMP SUBSCRIBE
rpcs.description: This RPC will maintain a list of patients & events to monitor for new data. The LIST of patients passed into this RPC is returned in ^TMP($J,"HMP",n) as XML, with a subscription status of 'on', 'off', or 'error'.

rpcs.name: HMP WRITEBACK PT DEM
rpcs.description: This RPC takes demographic data from an outside system and files it in to the Patient File (#2)

rpcs.name: HMPCPRS RPC
rpcs.description: USED TO RETURN HEADER INFORMATION TO MIMIC CPRS HEADERS IN EHMP GUI

rpcs.name: HMPCRPC RPC
rpcs.description: This RPC is used to save and get data from all the HMP PARAMETERS in all the  all the HMP parameter files.

rpcs.name: HMPCRPC RPCCHAIN
rpcs.description: Used to chain multiple HMP RPC's together. Flexible framework for invoking a RPC chain

rpcs.name: HMPDJFS API
rpcs.description: This is the primary entry point RPC for all the VX-Sync API. All sync related calls come through this entry point

rpcs.name: HMPDJFS DELSUB
rpcs.description: RPC used to delete eHMP subscriptions from file 800000

rpcs.name: HMPFPTC CHKS
rpcs.description: This RPC returns the patient selection checks for a sensitive patient,  deceased, and PRF.

rpcs.name: HMPFPTC LOG
rpcs.description: This RPC is used to log a patient when a provider is accessing a  sensitive record.

rpcs.name: HMP LOCAL CORRESPONDINGIDS
rpcs.description: Give a patient DFN, ICN, or EDIPI, this Remote Procedure Call returns a list of Treating Facilities, including SOURCE ID, SITE HASH, and IDENTIFIER STATUS.

rpcs.name: HMP PUT OPERATIONAL DATA
rpcs.description: This RPC accepts writeback data from JDS and returns a JSON formatted acknowledgement message.

rpcs.name: HMP PATIENT SCHED SYNC
rpcs.description: This RPC looks up patient appointments by date and location and returns  the following in a global array for patients that are not found in the  HMP SUBSCRIPTION file (#800000):       DFN^Appointment Date^Location Name^Location IEN

rpcs.name: HMP PATIENT ADMIT SYNC
rpcs.description: This RPC looks up patient admissions by location and returns the  following in a global array for patients that are not found in the HMP  SUBSCRIPTION file (#800000):       DFN^Admission Date^Location Name^Room-Bed^Location IEN       The Room-Bed may not be populated for all locations.

rpcs.name: HMP CHKXTMP
rpcs.description: This Remote Procedure returns information about the eHMP storage size  in ^XTMP and the number of patients in the queue.  The site hash plus  the local date and time are also returned.    No input parameters are required.

rpcs.name: HMP GLOBAL SIZE
rpcs.description: Remote procedure HMP GLOBAL SIZE takes no input parameters; it returns  the size of eHMP's current usage of the ^XTMP global.

rpcs.name: HMP DEFAULT PATIENT LIST
rpcs.description: This RPC will return the default patient list for a specific provider.  Input parameter is the provider's DUZ. Out put is an array with the  patient's DFN^Patient Name^Patient Location.

rpcs.name: SDEC ACCGROUP
rpcs.description: Return active entries from the SDEC ACCESS GROUP file 409.822

rpcs.name: SDEC ACCTYPE
rpcs.description: Return active entries from the SDEC ACCESS TYPE file 409.823

rpcs.name: SDEC PTLOOKRS
rpcs.description: GET Patients that match the partial lookup. The partial lookup can be a partial name, Date of Birth, Chart Number, SSN, or empty which will return all patients.

rpcs.name: SDEC WAITLIST
rpcs.description: Collect Wait List for a Given Resource

rpcs.name: SDEC UNREGEV
rpcs.description: Called by client to Unregister client's interest in SDECEVENT

rpcs.name: SDEC UNDOCANA
rpcs.description: UnCancel a Cancelled appointment.

rpcs.name: SDEC TPBLKOV
rpcs.description: TYPE BLOCKS OVERLAP

rpcs.name: SDEC SPACEBAR
rpcs.description: Update ^DISV with most recent lookup value SDECVAL from file SDECDIC

rpcs.name: SDEC SETFAC
rpcs.description: Remote procedure to Set the current facility/division for a given user.

rpcs.name: SDEC SEARCHAV
rpcs.description: Searches availability database for availability blocks between      SDECSTRT and SDECEND for each of the resources in SDECRES. The av blocks must be one of the types in SDECTYPES, must be  AM or PM depending on value in SDECAMPM and  must be on one of the weekdays listed in SDECWKDY.

rpcs.name: SDEC SUSRINFO
rpcs.description: SCHEDULING USER INFO

rpcs.name: SDEC RESOURCE
rpcs.description: Returns Recordset with ALL RESOURCE names. Inactive RESOURCES are NOT filtered out.

rpcs.name: SDEC RESUSER
rpcs.description: Returns entries from the SDEC RESOURCE USER file.

rpcs.name: SDEC RESLETRS
rpcs.description: Return recordset of RESOURCES and associated LETTERS. Used in generating rebook letters for a clinic.

rpcs.name: SDEC RESLETRF
rpcs.description: Return formatted text output of the Resource's Letter - either "LETTER  TEXT" (also used as Reminder Letter), NO SHOW LETTER, or CLINIC  CANCELLATION LETTER.

rpcs.name: SDEC RESGRPUS
rpcs.description: Returns ADO Recordset with all ACTIVE resource group names to which user  has access based on entries in SDEC RESOURCE USER file If SDECDUZ=0 then returns all department names for current DUZ If user SDECDUZ possesses the key SDECZMGR then ALL resource group names are returned regardless of whether

rpcs.name: SDEC REGEVENT
rpcs.description: Called by client to inform the server of client's interest in SDECEVENT

rpcs.name: SDEC SETRBOOK
rpcs.description: Sets rebook date into appointment

rpcs.name: SDEC REBKNEXT
rpcs.description: GET - find next ACCESS BLOCK in resource SDECRES after SDECSTART.

rpcs.name: SDEC REBKLIST
rpcs.description: Return patient appointments used in listing Re-Booked appointments.

rpcs.name: SDEC REBKCLIN
rpcs.description: Return recordset of rebooked patient appointments between dates SDECBEG and SDECEND for each clinic in SDECCLST. Used in generating rebook letters for a clinic SDECCLST is a |-delimited list of SDEC RESOURCE iens.   (The last |-piece is null, so discard it.) SDECBEG and SDECEND are in external date

rpcs.name: SDEC RAISEVNT
rpcs.description: Raise Event

rpcs.name: SDEC PROVCLIN
rpcs.description: Remote procedure to return the Providers that are assigned to a given  Clinic.

rpcs.name: SDEC APPTLETR
rpcs.description: Print Appointment Letter. Print appointment letter. Currently supports No-Show, Cancel by Patient,  and Cancel by Clinic.

rpcs.name: SDEC PATAPPTH
rpcs.description: Remote procedure to return the Patient appointment history in formatted  text.

rpcs.name: SDEC PATAPPTD
rpcs.description: Return recordset of patient appointments used in listing a patient's appointments and generating patient letters.

rpcs.name: SDEC OVBOOK
rpcs.description: Check if Overbook is allowed for given Clinic and Date.

rpcs.name: SDEC NOSHOPAT
rpcs.description: COLLECT NO-SHOW DATA for Patient

rpcs.name: SDEC NOSHOW
rpcs.description: Sets appointment noshow flag in SDEC APPOINTMENT file

rpcs.name: SDEC NEWPERS
rpcs.description: return entries from the NEW PERSON table 200 that are 'active' AND have  PROVIDER CLASS defined.

rpcs.name: SDEC WLSET
rpcs.description: Waitlist Set

rpcs.name: SDEC WLGET
rpcs.description: Waitlist GET

rpcs.name: SDEC WLCLOSE
rpcs.description: Remove a Patient from the Wait List by setting the STATUS to CLOSED  and updating the DISPOSITION fields.

rpcs.name: SDEC SVSPALL
rpcs.description: Return the IEN and NAME for all entries in the SD WL SERVICE/SPECIALTY  file
